name: CuPy
summary: Drop-in GPU replacement for NumPy using CUDA, providing the same API surface with GPU-accelerated array operations for NVIDIA hardware.

sections.name: Array Creation

functions.purpose: Create a GPU array from a Python list or NumPy array
Return a new GPU array of given shape filled with zeros
Return evenly spaced values within a given interval on the GPU
Return a 2-D identity matrix on the GPU

gotchas: Transferring data from CPU to GPU with cp.array() is synchronous and blocks the host until the copy completes; batch transfers to minimize overhead.
cp.empty() allocates uninitialized GPU memory and may contain garbage values from previous allocations; always initialize before use.

sections.name: Math Operations

functions.purpose: Sum of array elements over a given axis on the GPU
Compute the arithmetic mean along the specified axis
Calculate the exponential of all elements in the input array
Dot product of two arrays on the GPU

gotchas: Integer division on CuPy follows the same behavior as NumPy; use cp.true_divide() to ensure float output when dividing integers.
Reduction operations like cp.sum() and cp.mean() return a 0-d GPU array, not a Python scalar; call .item() to transfer a single value to the host.

sections.name: Linear Algebra

functions.purpose: Compute the inverse of a matrix on the GPU
Solve a linear matrix equation on the GPU
Compute the eigenvalues of a general matrix
Singular value decomposition on the GPU

gotchas: cp.linalg.solve() requires the coefficient matrix to be square and non-singular; singular matrices raise LinAlgError without a clear fallback.
Large SVD operations on the GPU can exhaust memory because intermediate buffers for U, S, and Vh are all allocated simultaneously; consider truncated SVD for tall matrices.

sections.name: FFT

functions.purpose: Compute the one-dimensional discrete Fourier Transform on the GPU
Compute the inverse one-dimensional discrete Fourier Transform
Compute the 2-dimensional discrete Fourier Transform on the GPU
Shift the zero-frequency component to the center of the spectrum

gotchas: FFT output length is determined by the input shape; padding or truncating with the n parameter changes the frequency resolution without warning.
cp.fft.fft() operates on the last axis by default; for batch FFT over a specific axis, use axis=1 explicitly to avoid silent dimension mismatches.
